heading: Imposition of sanctions with respect to human rights abuses and perpetration of a coup in Burma
label: 10222 Imposition of sanctions with respect to human rights abuses and perpetration of a coup in Burma

children.label: A
children.text: the Burmese military or security forces of Burma;

children.label: B
children.text: the State Administration Council, the military-appointed cabinet at the level of Deputy Minister or higher, or a military-appointed minister of a Burmese state or region; or

children.label: C
children.text: an entity that primarily operates in the defense sector of the Burmese economy; or

children.label: A
children.text: is operating in the industrial or extractive sectors; and

children.label: B
children.text: significantly financially benefits the Burmese military.

children.heading: Additional measure relating to facilitation of transactions
children.label: b Additional measure relating to facilitation of transactions
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may, in consultation with the Secretary of State, prohibit or impose strict conditions on the opening or maintaining in the United States of a correspondent account or payable-through account by a foreign financial institution that the President determines has, on or after , knowingly conducted or facilitated a significant transaction or transactions on behalf of a foreign person subject to sanctions under this section imposed pursuant to subsection (a).December 23, 2022
the Myanma Oil and Gas Enterprise;

children.label: A
children.text: is not operating in the industrial or extractive sectors; and

children.label: B
children.text: significantly financially benefits the Burmese military;
a spouse or adult child of any person described in subsection (a)(1);

children.label: A
children.text: actions or policies that significantly undermine democratic processes or institutions in Burma;

children.label: B
children.text: actions or policies that significantly threaten the peace, security, or stability of Burma;

children.label: i
children.text: significantly prohibit, limit, or penalize the exercise of freedom of expression or assembly by people in Burma; or

children.label: ii
children.text: limit access to print, online, or broadcast media in Burma; or

children.label: D
children.text: the orchestration of arbitrary detention or torture in Burma or other serious human rights abuses in Burma; or
any Burmese entity that provides materiel to the Burmese military.

children.heading: Property blocking
children.label: 1 Property blocking
children.text: The President may exercise all powers granted to the President by the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) to the extent necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of the foreign person if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Foreign exchange
children.label: 2 Foreign exchange
children.text: The President may, pursuant to such regulations as the President may prescribe, prohibit any transactions in foreign exchange that are subject to the jurisdiction of the United States and in which the foreign person has any interest.

children.label: i
children.text: inadmissible to the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: ineligible for a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).8 U.S.C. 1101

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The issuing consular officer, the Secretary of State, or the Secretary of Homeland Security (or a designee of one of such Secretaries) shall, in accordance with section 221(i) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (), revoke any visa or other entry documentation issued to an alien described in subparagraph (A) regardless of when the visa or other entry documentation is issued.8 U.S.C. 1201(i)

children.label: I
children.text: shall take effect immediately; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall automatically cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the alien’s possession.

children.heading: Assessment and report on sanctions with respect to Burmese State-owned enterprise operating in the energy sector
children.label: e Assessment and report on sanctions with respect to Burmese State-owned enterprise operating in the energy sector

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the President shall conduct an assessment with respect to the Burmese state-owned enterprise described in subsection (c)(1), including relevant factors pertaining to the possible application of sanctions on such enterprise.December 23, 2022

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 2 Report required
children.text: Upon making the determination required by paragraph (1), the President shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the assessment.

children.heading: Form of report
children.label: 3 Form of report
children.text: The report required by paragraph (2) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may include a classified annex.

children.heading: Exception for intelligence, law enforcement, and national security activities
children.label: 1 Exception for intelligence, law enforcement, and national security activities
children.text: Sanctions under this section shall not apply to any authorized intelligence, law enforcement, or national security activities of the United States.

children.heading: Exception to comply with international obligations
children.label: 2 Exception to comply with international obligations
children.text: Sanctions under subsection (d)(3) shall not apply with respect to the admission of an alien if admitting or paroling the alien into the United States is necessary to permit the United States to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, or other applicable international obligations.June 26, 1947November 21, 1947

children.heading: Exception relating to the provision of humanitarian assistance
children.label: 3 Exception relating to the provision of humanitarian assistance
A
children.text: the sale of agricultural commodities, food, medicine, or medical devices to Burma;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of humanitarian assistance to the people of Burma;

children.label: C
children.text: financial transactions relating to humanitarian assistance or for humanitarian purposes in Burma; or

children.label: D
children.text: transporting goods or services that are necessary to carry out operations relating to humanitarian assistance or humanitarian purposes in Burma.

children.heading: Exception relating to wind-down of projects
children.label: 4 Exception relating to wind-down of projects
A
children.text: agreements entered into between United States persons and the Government of Burma prior to ;May 21, 1997

children.label: B
children.text: the exercise of rights pursuant to such agreements; or

children.label: C
children.text: transactions related to the subsequent operation of the assets encompassed by such disposed investments.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: g Waiver
children.text: The President may, on a case-by-case basis waive the application of sanctions or restrictions imposed with respect to a foreign person under this section if the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees at the time such waiver is to take effect that the waiver is in the national interest of the United States.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided to the President under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out this section.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: The penalties provided for in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () shall apply to a person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of this section or any regulations promulgated under this section to the same extent that such penalties apply to a person that commits an unlawful act described in section 206(a) of that Act [].50 U.S.C. 170550 U.S.C. 1705(a)
describes the primary sources of income to which the Burmese military has access and that the United States has been unable to reach using sanctions authorities; and
assesses the impact of the sanctions imposed pursuant to the authorities under this section on the Burmese people and the Burmese military.
